channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Reggie Fils-Aimé Explains Nintendo’s Stance on Layoffs
item.content:encoded:
Reggie Fils-Aimé, the former president of Nintendo of America, recently shed some light on the way the company viewed mass layoffs during his time there. This comes at a time when mass layoffs are very common in the gaming industry.

Reggie Fils-Aimé was president of Nintendo of America from 2006 to 2019. During that time, he oversaw the success of consoles such as the Nintendo Wii and the launch of the original Nintendo Switch.

Nintendo’s Viewpoint on Layoffs Reaffirmed

Also during Reggie’s tenure, there was the less-than-stellar run of the Nintendo Wii U. Rather than immediately resorting to layoffs, the Nintendo Company Limited president at the time, Satoru Iwata, took a 50% pay cut in 2014 after Nintendo’s profits fell amid weak Wii U performance, and the reduction was set to last five months.

At the time, Iwata shared his belief that layoffs would hurt the company’s morale and weaken its ability to make great games.

During a recent visit to NYU, Reggie was interviewed about his time at Nintendo. As part of that discussion, Reggie discussed the company’s view on layoffs, saying that mass layoffs are not something the brand has ever been interested in.

“We thought about people from the standpoint that we never wanted to go through layoffs or mass eliminations of people. It just wasn’t in our DNA,” he said (via DualShockers).

At a time when Epic Games has laid off more than 1,000 people and several gaming projects have shut down due to quick returns on investment, Nintendo has avoided many of these pitfalls and continues to march to the beat of its own drum, pumping out exclusives and transitioning its largest properties into media juggernauts beyond the world of video games.

Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Miyamoto Discusses Princess Peach’s Growth in Mario Movies
item.content:encoded:
Shigeru Miyamoto comments on Princess Peach’s character growth in the Super Mario Bros. movie duology. Miyamoto is, of course, the creator of all of the characters in the Mario universe, but until she appeared on the big screen, Peach’s role has largely been reserved for being the damsel in distress that Mario has to save from Bowser.

In recent movies, Peach has taken on a much more confident role, playing more of a heroine than someone who needs rescuing. Shigeru Miyamoto is now discussing why the function required of the princess of the Mushroom Kingdom changes when crafting the story of a film.

Miyamoto Addresses Peach’s Shift From Damsel to Hero

In a recent interview with Japan’s Oricon, Miyamoto explained that the mindset going into the Super Mario Galaxy Movie in 2026 was different than even the mindsets surrounding Peach in the original Super Mario Bros. Movie from 2023.

“Clarity is very important in games, and players need to be able to understand what they need to do immediately. That’s why the structure of ‘going to rescue the kidnapped princess’ has functioned as a kind of symbol.” He frankly stated, “I understood that there were criticisms that this didn’t fit with the current image of women. However, it’s also a part of the game’s mechanics that can’t be easily changed.”

　He then switched his focus to discussing the filmmaking mindset: “We decided to make a bold change, and had many discussions with Chris and others. In the first film, we clearly presented the ‘fighting woman,’ but in the second film, we felt that wasn’t enough. By depicting Peach’s worries and emotions, and the fluctuations in her relationship with Mario, I think she became a richer character.”

The recent movie actually made a major change to Princess Peach’s lore. Those changes might carry over into future video games, as Miyamoto has previously commented. Check out those comments here.

What do you think about Miyamoto’s comments concerning the growth of the Princess Peach character? Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Gamers May Get Refunds After Sony PlayStation Digital Store Lawsuit
item.content:encoded:
Sony PlayStation players may be eligible for reimbursement following a preliminary settlement agreement approved by a judge in a class-action lawsuit regarding digital game purchases.

The Northern District of California recently approved this preliminary settlement against Sony. The class-action lawsuit, filed in 2023, accuses Sony of monopolizing the market by restricting third-party games on their platform.

Sony PlayStation Digital Game Purchase Settlement Reached

As part of the settlement, Sony is required to pay approximately $7.8 million to all in the United States who purchased specific digital games through the PlayStation Network (PSN) between April 1, 2019, and December 31, 2023. The settlement was officially approved on April 8. The Saveri Law Firm officially announced the settlement through a press release on April 29.

“The Court has preliminarily approved the proposed settlement between the Plaintiffs and Defendant (the “Settlement”). The proposed Settlement will provide for the payment of $7,850,000 (the “Settlement Amount”) to resolve Plaintiffs’ and Settlement Class Members’ claims against Defendant,” the press release reads.

Eligible purchases that make customers available for reimbursement include WWE 2K17, WWE 2K18, WWE 2K19, No Man’s Sky, Super Mega Baseball, The Last of Us Remastered, and Until Dawn. Also included are NBA 2K18, NBA 2K19, the Mass Effect Trilogy, Madden NFL 17, and more. Check out the full list of eligible games at this link.

What do you think about this preliminary settlement agreement? Are you eligible for reimbursement based on purchases you’ve made? Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
